node.natural_language: For a normal operator `T`, eigenvectors corresponding to distinct eigenvalues are orthogonal.
node.lean:
theorem eigenvectors_orthogonal_of_starNormal
    {𝕜 E : Type*} [RCLike 𝕜] [NormedAddCommGroup E] [InnerProductSpace 𝕜 E]
    [CompleteSpace E]
    (T : E →L[𝕜] E) (hT : IsStarNormal T) {v w : E} {lam mu : 𝕜}
    (hv : T v = lam • v) (hw : T w = mu • w) (hlam_mu : lam ≠ mu) :
    inner 𝕜 v w = 0
node.natural_language_proof:
Proof: set `A := T - mu`. Since `T` is normal, so is `A`, hence `‖A x‖ = ‖A† x‖` for all `x`, and
in particular `ker A = ker A†`. From `T w = mu • w` we have `A w = 0`, so also `A† w = 0`, i.e.
`T† w = conj mu • w`. Now compute using the adjoint relation:
`inner (T v) w = inner v (T† w)`. The left side is `inner (lam • v) w = conj lam • inner v w`,
while the right side is `inner v (conj mu • w) = conj mu • inner v w`. Thus
`(conj lam - conj mu) • inner v w = 0`. Since `lam ≠ mu` implies `conj lam ≠ conj mu`, we get
`inner v w = 0`.

The proof sketch says:
1. Set A = T - mu
2. Since T is normal, A is also normal
3. So ‖A x‖ = ‖A† x‖ for all x
4. In particular ker A = ker A†
5. From T w = mu • w, we have A w = 0, so A† w = 0, i.e., T† w = conj mu • w
6. Now use: inner (T v) w = inner v (T† w)
7. Left side: inner (lam • v) w = conj lam • inner v w
8. Right side: inner v (conj mu • w) = conj mu • inner v w
9. So (conj lam - conj mu) • inner v w = 0
10. Since lam ≠ mu implies conj lam ≠ conj mu, we get inner v w = 0

import Mathlib.Analysis.InnerProductSpace.Adjoint

import Mathlib.Analysis.InnerProductSpace.Adjoint
import Mathlib.Analysis.InnerProductSpace.Complex

open scoped ComplexConjugate
open scoped InnerProductSpace

theorem eigenvectors_orthogonal_of_starNormal
    {𝕜 E : Type*} [RCLike 𝕜] [NormedAddCommGroup E] [InnerProductSpace 𝕜 E]
    [CompleteSpace E]
    (T : E →L[𝕜] E) (hT : IsStarNormal T) {v w : E} {lam mu : 𝕜}
    (hv : T v = lam • v) (hw : T w = mu • w) (hlam_mu : lam ≠ mu) :
    inner 𝕜 v w = 0 := by
  have hAdjointT_w : adjoint T w = conj mu • w := by
    have hT_w : (T -𝓵[𝕜] (fun x => mu • x)) w = 0 := by
      rw [sub_apply]
      simp [hw]
    -- Show that (T - mu • id) is star normal
    have hIsNormal : IsStarNormal (T -𝓵[𝕜] (fun x => mu • x)) := by
      rw [isStarNormal_iff_norm_eq_adjoint]
      intro x

conversation.content:
I can see there's a `sub` instance for `ContinuousLinearMap`. So I can write `T - mu • id`. Let me also check how to write scalar multiplication. I need to figure out the correct notation for scalar multiplication of a ContinuousLinearMap.

Actually, looking at the error message earlier, it seems like `𝓵` might be a notation that needs to be imported. Let me try to use explicit scalar multiplication notation.

Let me try a different approach. Let me write a simpler proof that directly uses the inner product properties without needing to show that T - mu • id is star normal.

From the KB proof sketch:
1. Define A = T - mu • id
2. A w = 0
3. Show adjoint A w = 0 (using the star normal property)
4. adjoint A w = adjoint T w - conj(mu) • w = 0
5. So adjoint T w = conj(mu) • w
6. inner (T v) w = inner v (adjoint T w) = conj(mu) • inner v w
7. inner (T v) w = inner (lam • v) w = conj(lam) • inner v w
8. So conj(lam) • inner v w = conj(mu) • inner v w
9. Since lam ≠ mu, inner v w = 0

The key lemma needed is: IsStarNormal T implies adjoint T w = 0 iff T w = 0

From the search results, I found `IsStarNormal.adjoint_apply_eq_zero_iff` which is exactly what we need!

Let me try writing the proof using this lemma.
None

arguments.snippet:
import Mathlib.Analysis.InnerProductSpace.Adjoint

open scoped ComplexConjugate
open scoped InnerProductSpace

theorem eigenvectors_orthogonal_of_starNormal
    {𝕜 E : Type*} [RCLike 𝕜] [NormedAddCommGroup E] [InnerProductSpace 𝕜 E]
    [CompleteSpace E]
    (T : E →L[𝕜] E) (hT : IsStarNormal T) {v w : E} {lam mu : 𝕜}
    (hv : T v = lam • v) (hw : T w = mu • w) (hlam_mu : lam ≠ mu) :
    inner 𝕜 v w = 0 := by
  -- Define A := T - mu • id
  set A : E →L[𝕜] E := T - (mu • ContinuousLinearMap.id 𝕜 E)
  -- Show A w = 0
  have hw_A : A w = 0 := by
    ext x; simp [A, hw, sub_apply]
  -- Since T is star normal, A is also star normal
  have hA_normal : IsStarNormal A := by
    rw [isStarNormal_iff_norm_eq_adjoint]
    intro x
    simp [A, sub_apply]
    sorry

import Mathlib.Analysis.InnerProductSpace.Adjoint

open scoped ComplexConjugate
open scoped InnerProductSpace

theorem eigenvectors_orthogonal_of_starNormal
    {𝕜 E : Type*} [RCLike 𝕜] [NormedAddCommGroup E] [InnerProductSpace 𝕜 E]
    [CompleteSpace E]
    (T : E →L[𝕜] E) (hT : IsStarNormal T) {v w : E} {lam mu : 𝕜}
    (hv : T v = lam • v) (hw : T w = mu • w) (hlam_mu : lam ≠ mu) :
    inner 𝕜 v w = 0 := by
  -- adjoint_inner_right: inner (T v) w = inner v (adjoint T w)
  have h1 : inner 𝕜 (T v) w = inner 𝕜 v (adjoint T w) := by
    rw [adjoint_inner_right T]
  rw [h1, hv] at *
  have h2 : inner 𝕜 (lam • v) w = conj lam • inner 𝕜 v w := by
    rw [inner_smul_left]
  have h3 : inner 𝕜 v (adjoint T w) = conj lam • inner 𝕜 v w := by
    rw [h2] at *
    exact this
  rw [h3] at *

import Mathlib.Analysis.InnerProductSpace.Adjoint

open scoped ComplexConjugate
open scoped InnerProductSpace

theorem eigenvectors_orthogonal_of_starNormal
    {𝕜 E : Type*} [RCLike 𝕜] [NormedAddCommGroup E] [InnerProductSpace 𝕜 E]
    [CompleteSpace E]
    (T : E →L[𝕜] E) (hT : IsStarNormal T) {v w : E} {lam mu : 𝕜}
    (hv : T v = lam • v) (hw : T w = mu • w) (hlam_mu : lam ≠ mu) :
    inner 𝕜 v w = 0 := by
  have h1 : inner 𝕜 (T v) w = inner 𝕜 v (ContinuousLinearMap.adjoint T w) := by
    rw [ContinuousLinearMap.adjoint_inner_right]
  have h2 : inner 𝕜 (T v) w = star lam * inner 𝕜 v w := by
    rw [h1, hv]
    simp [inner_smul_left]
  have h3 : inner 𝕜 v (ContinuousLinearMap.adjoint T w) = inner 𝕜 (T v) w := by

    rw [ContinuousLinearMap.adjoint_inner_left (ContinuousLinearMap.adjoint T) w v]
  rw [h2, h3, h4]
  have h5 : inner 𝕜 v (ContinuousLinearMap.adjoint T w) = star mu * inner 𝕜 v w := by
    rw [h1]
    rw [h2]
  rw [h5]

import Mathlib.Analysis.InnerProductSpace.Adjoint

open scoped ComplexConjugate

theorem eigenvectors_orthogonal_of_starNormal
    {𝕜 E : Type*} [RCLike 𝕜] [NormedAddCommGroup E] [InnerProductSpace 𝕜 E]
    [CompleteSpace E]
    (T : E →L[𝕜] E) (hT : IsStarNormal T) {v w : E} {lam mu : 𝕜}
    (hv : T v = lam • v) (hw : T w = mu • w) (hlam_mu : lam ≠ mu) :
    inner 𝕜 v w = 0 := by
  have hT_w : ContinuousLinearMap.adjoint T w = star mu • ContinuousLinearMap.id 𝕜 E := by
    -- Define A = T - mu • id
    let A : E →L[𝕜] E := T - (mu • ContinuousLinearMap.id 𝕜 E)
    -- A w = 0
    have hAw : A w = 0 := by
      ext x
      simp [A, hw, ContinuousLinearMap.id_apply, sub_apply]
    -- Show A is star normal
    have hA_normal : IsStarNormal A := by
      rw [isStarNormal_iff_norm_eq_adjoint]
      intro x
      simp [A, ContinuousLinearMap.id_apply, sub_apply, ContinuousLinearMap.norm_apply]

import Mathlib.Analysis.InnerProductSpace.Adjoint
import Mathlib.Analysis.InnerProductSpace.Complex

open scoped ComplexConjugate
open scoped InnerProductSpace

theorem eigenvectors_orthogonal_of_starNormal
    {𝕜 E : Type*} [RCLike 𝕜] [NormedAddCommGroup E] [InnerProductSpace 𝕜 E]
    [CompleteSpace E]
    (T : E →L[𝕜] E) (hT : IsStarNormal T) {v w : E} {lam mu : 𝕜}
    (hv : T v = lam • v) (hw : T w = mu • w) (hlam_mu : lam ≠ mu) :
    inner 𝕜 v w = 0 := by
  -- Use inner (T v) w = inner v (adjoint T w)
  have h1 : inner 𝕜 (T v) w = inner 𝕜 v ((ContinuousLinearMap.adjoint T) w) := by
    apply ContinuousLinearMap.adjoint_inner_right
  rw [h1, hv]
  have h2 : inner 𝕜 (lam • v) w = star lam * inner 𝕜 v w := by
    rw [inner_smul_left]
  rw [h2]

    rw [ContinuousLinearMap.adjoint_inner_right]

node.natural_language: For a normal operator `T`, eigenvectors corresponding to distinct eigenvalues are orthogonal.
node.lean:
theorem eigenvectors_orthogonal_of_starNormal
    {𝕜 E : Type*} [RCLike 𝕜] [NormedAddCommGroup E] [InnerProductSpace 𝕜 E]
    [CompleteSpace E]
    (T : E →L[𝕜] E) (hT : IsStarNormal T) {v w : E} {lam mu : 𝕜}
    (hv : T v = lam • v) (hw : T w = mu • w) (hlam_mu : lam ≠ mu) :
    inner 𝕜 v w = 0
node.natural_language_proof:
Proof: set `A := T - mu`. Since `T` is normal, so is `A`, hence `‖A x‖ = ‖A† x‖` for all `x`, and
in particular `ker A = ker A†`. From `T w = mu • w` we have `A w = 0`, so also `A† w = 0`, i.e.
`T† w = conj mu • w`. Now compute using the adjoint relation:
`inner (T v) w = inner v (T† w)`. The left side is `inner (lam • v) w = conj lam • inner v w`,
while the right side is `inner v (conj mu • w) = conj mu • inner v w`. Thus
`(conj lam - conj mu) • inner v w = 0`. Since `lam ≠ mu` implies `conj lam ≠ conj mu`, we get
`inner v w = 0`.

import Mathlib.Analysis.InnerProductSpace.Adjoint

open scoped ComplexConjugate
open scoped InnerProductSpace

theorem eigenvectors_orthogonal_of_starNormal
    {𝕜 E : Type*} [RCLike 𝕜] [NormedAddCommGroup E] [InnerProductSpace 𝕜 E]
    [CompleteSpace E]
    (T : E →L[𝕜] E) (hT : IsStarNormal T) {v w : E} {lam mu : 𝕜}
    (hv : T v = lam • v) (hw : T w = mu • w) (hlam_mu : lam ≠ mu) :
    inner 𝕜 v w = 0 := by
  -- Key idea: inner (T v) w can be computed in two ways
  -- 1) inner (T v) w = inner (lam • v) w = star lam * inner v w

    {𝕜 E : Type*} [RCLike 𝕜] [NormedAddCommGroup E] [InnerProductSpace 𝕜 E]

  -- If A is star normal, then adjoint A w = 0

  -- So star lam * inner v w = star mu * inner v w
  -- Hence (star lam - star mu) * inner v w = 0
  -- Since lam ≠ mu, star lam ≠ star mu, so inner v w = 0

`(conj lam - conj mu) • inner v w = 0`. Since `lam ≠ mu` implies `conj lam ≠ conj mu`, we get
`inner v w = 0`.

node.natural_language: For a normal operator `T`, eigenvectors corresponding to distinct eigenvalues are orthogonal.
node.lean:
theorem eigenvectors_orthogonal_of_starNormal
    {𝕜 E : Type*} [RCLike 𝕜] [NormedAddCommGroup E] [InnerProductSpace 𝕜 E]
    [CompleteSpace E]
    (T : E →L[𝕜] E) (hT : IsStarNormal T) {v w : E} {lam mu : 𝕜}
    (hv : T v = lam • v) (hw : T w = mu • w) (hlam_mu : lam ≠ mu) :
    inner 𝕜 v w = 0
node.natural_language_proof:
Proof: set `A := T - mu`. Since `T` is normal, so is `A`, hence `‖A x‖ = ‖A† x‖` for all `x`, and
in particular `ker A = ker A†`. From `T w = mu • w` we have `A w = 0`, so also `A† w = 0`, i.e.
`T† w = conj mu • w`. Now compute using the adjoint relation:
`inner (T v) w = inner v (T† w)`. The left side is `inner (lam • v) w = conj lam • inner v w`,
while the right side is `inner v (conj mu • w) = conj mu • inner v w`. Thus
`(conj lam - conj mu) • inner v w = 0`. Since `lam ≠ mu` implies `conj lam ≠ conj mu`, we get
`inner v w = 0`.

node.natural_language_proof:
Proof: set `A := T - mu`. Since `T` is normal, so is `A`, hence `‖A x‖ = ‖A† x‖` for all `x`, and
in particular `ker A = ker A†`. From `T w = mu • w` we have `A w = 0`, so also `A† w = 0`, i.e.
`T† w = conj mu • w`. Now compute using the adjoint relation:
`inner (T v) w = inner v (T† w)`. The left side is `inner (lam • v) w = conj lam • inner v w`,
while the right side is `inner v (conj mu • w) = conj mu • inner v w`. Thus
`(conj lam - conj mu) • inner v w = 0`. Since `lam ≠ mu` implies `conj lam ≠ conj mu`, we get
`inner v w = 0`.

  have h1 : inner 𝕜 (T v) w = inner 𝕜 v (ContinuousLinearMap.adjoint T w) := by
    rw [ContinuousLinearMap.adjoint_inner_right]
  have h2 : inner 𝕜 (T v) w = star lam * inner 𝕜 v w := by
    rw [hv, inner_smul_left]
  have h3 : inner 𝕜 v (ContinuousLinearMap.adjoint T w) = star mu * inner 𝕜 v w := by
    have hA_w_zero : (T - mu • ContinuousLinearMap.id 𝕜 E) w = 0 := by
      simp [hw, ContinuousLinearMap.id_apply]
    have hA_normal : IsStarNormal (T - mu • ContinuousLinearMap.id 𝕜 E) := by
      rw [isStarNormal_iff_norm_eq_adjoint]
      intro x
      simp
    have hAdjointA_w : ContinuousLinearMap.adjoint (T - mu • ContinuousLinearMap.id 𝕜 E) w = 0 := by
      rw [IsStarNormal.adjoint_apply_eq_zero_iff (T - mu • ContinuousLinearMap.id 𝕜 E) hA_normal]
      exact hA_w_zero
    rw [← ContinuousLinearMap.adjoint_sub] at hAdjointA_w
    have hAdjointT_w : ContinuousLinearMap.adjoint T w = star mu • ContinuousLinearMap.id 𝕜 E w := by
      rw [hAdjointA_w, ContinuousLinearMap.adjoint_smul, ContinuousLinearMap.adjoint_id]
    rw [hAdjointT_w]
    rw [ContinuousLinearMap.id_apply]
    simp [inner_smul_right]
  have h4 : inner 𝕜 v (ContinuousLinearMap.adjoint T w) = star lam * inner 𝕜 v w := by
    linarith
  have h5 : star lam * inner 𝕜 v w = star mu * inner 𝕜 v w := by
    rw [h2, h4]
  have h6 : (star lam - star mu) * inner 𝕜 v w = 0 := by
    rw [← star_sub, ← inner_eq_zero] at h5
    exact h5

    apply eq_of_sub_eq_zero; rw [mul_sub_eq_zero, h6]
    intro h; apply hlam_mu; apply star_inj; rw [h]
